tasks.name: Executing Git Backup
tasks.system_prompt: You are an automation agent responsible for performing git backups and notifying the user of results. Execute tasks reliably and report outcomes clearly.
tasks.prompt:
Execute the git-backup skill by running the backup script at /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py, then use the notify-user skill to send a notification with the backup result.

Steps:
1. Clean up all of the task history if the last run is successful. This is critical to keep our context minimal without losing important details!
2. Run: python /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py
3. Capture the output/result
4. Send notification using: python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "<backup result message>" --title "Git Backup Result" --priority 3
Always report whether the backup succeeded or failed in the notification.

tasks.name: Blog Ideas
tasks.system_prompt: You are a blog topic researcher for Able.SG, a tech leadership blog by Michael Bui - a Singapore-based engineering leader who runs an extensive homelab, builds multi-agent AI systems, and leads AI transformation at a large retail organization.
tasks.prompt:
This task has 4 steps. Follow them in exact order.

== STEP 1: Extract Published Blog Titles and Date Range ==

Run this shell command to get published (non-draft) blog post titles AND the search date range:

bash -c '
REPO_DIR="/sources/github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG"
if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/.git" ]; then
  git -C "$REPO_DIR" pull --quiet 2>/dev/null
else
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$REPO_DIR")"
  git clone https://github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG.git "$REPO_DIR" --depth 1 --quiet 2>/dev/null
fi
echo "=== PUBLISHED POSTS ==="
count=0
for f in "$REPO_DIR"/content/english/posts/*.md; do
  draft=$(grep -m1 "^draft:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | tr -d " " | cut -d: -f2)
  [ "$draft" = "true" ] && continue
  title=$(grep -m1 "^title:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | sed "s/^title: *//;s/\"//g")
  if [ -n "$title" ]; then
    echo "- $title"
    count=$((count + 1))
  fi
done
if [ "$count" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "WARNING: No blog titles found. Check if /sources mount is available."
fi
echo ""
echo "=== SEARCH DATE RANGE ==="
THIS_MONTH="$(date +"%B %Y")"
PREV_MONTH="$(date -d "last month" +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null || date -v-1m +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null)"
echo "Search for topics trending in: $PREV_MONTH and $THIS_MONTH"
echo "⚠️ CRITICAL: All search queries must end with \" since $PREV_MONTH\" keywords"
'

Save the output. The PUBLISHED_POSTS section lists posts to avoid duplicating. The SEARCH DATE RANGE gives you two months to include in your search queries.

== STEP 2: Research Topics (run at least 7 web searches) ==

You must perform AT LEAST 7 separate web searches using search_engine before proposing topics. Design your own search queries, but follow these rules:

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Minimum 7 searches total, each with a DIFFERENT query
- At least 2 searches for Leadership topics (people management, engineering culture, AI adoption, trust, growth)
- At least 3 searches for Technology topics (agentic AI, local LLMs, homelab, MCP, clean architecture, DORA)
- At least 2 searches for Life topics (productivity, work-life balance, continuous learning, personal growth)
- Each search must approach the pillar from a DIFFERENT angle (e.g. for Technology: one about trends, one about community discussions, one about practical implementations)
- Include the month names and year from the SEARCH DATE RANGE (Step 1 output) in your queries to limit results to the last 2 months
- If a search returns interesting leads, do follow-up searches to go deeper

Execute all searches one by one, then analyze the results against Michael's expertise below.

MICHAEL'S UNIQUE EXPERTISE (real hands-on experience):
- Multi-agent orchestration (built 2 production systems: enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report)
- Agent Zero framework (runs 11 specialist agents, built custom theme plugin, skills for Gmail/Jira/GChat via CDP browser)
- AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server (local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp)
- Retail media networks / ad-tech (scaled from $1M to $10M+ GMV at NTUC FairPrice)
- DORA metrics, SLO management, engineering culture (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%)
- AI adoption at organizational scale (drove 28% to 100% department-wide, 2/3 building agentic AI)
- Clean architecture in Go microservices (SOLID, 12-factor app, SonarCloud quality gates)
- Terraform IaC for homelab (Proxmox VMs, Docker containers, NFS)
- RAG evaluation (ColPali multi-vector vs sparse vector, error rates, normalization)
- Cost optimization ($100K+ annual cloud savings: Datadog $70K + GCP + SOLR legacy removal)
- Team leadership (up to 14 cross-functional members, mentored 5 engineers to team lead)
- Homelab migration (Kubernetes + ArgoCD + Cloudflare to Docker + Terraform + Traefik, 30% power reduction)
- Prompt engineering and context engineering for production agentic systems
- Vendor evaluation (CitrusAd, Mirakl, MoEngage, Algolia, theTradeDesk, OSMOS, Segment)

TOPIC SELECTION RULES:
1. Do NOT suggest topics overlapping with PUBLISHED_POSTS from Step 1
2. Each topic MUST connect to at least one item from Michael's expertise above
3. Prioritize topics where personal experience gives an angle generic AI content cannot replicate
4. No purely theoretical topics - must have a hands-on component
5. Target audience: mid-to-senior engineers, tech leads, engineering managers in APAC/Singapore
6. Each topic should be writable as 800-1200 words

After all searches are complete, decide on 5-7 topics. For each, pick a TITLE and a SCORE (1-10, composite of search demand + uniqueness + freshness). Keep these in mind for Step 3.

== STEP 3: Save Topics to File, Then Notify ==

⚠️ CRITICAL: This step has two parts, you MUST execute BOTH before moving to Step 4.

PART A - SAVE TO FILE:
Create the output directory and save all topics with full details to a markdown file. Run:

mkdir -p "workdir/Blog Ideas"

Then write a file at workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_YYYY-MM-DD.md (replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's date). The file must follow this structure:

# Blog Topic Ideas - YYYY-MM-DD

## 1. [TITLE]
- **Pillar:** [Leadership / Technology / Life]
- **Score:** [X/10]
- **Angle:** [3-5 sentences on what makes Michael's take unique]
- **Trending Signal:** [detailed findings from web search with links to actual sources]
- **Related Expertise:** [which items from Michael's expertise list this connects to]

## 2. [TITLE]
(same structure for each topic)

PART B - SEND NOTIFICATION:
After saving the file, send a push notification with titles and scores. Execute:

python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "Blog Topics: (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | ... Check Agent Zero chat for details." --title "Blog Topic Ideas" --priority 3

Replace each SCORE and TITLE with actual values from your topics. Include ALL topics separated by " | ".

Execute both Part A and Part B now before proceeding to Step 4.

== STEP 4: Write Detailed Output ==

Now write the full details for each topic in this format:

[NUMBER]. TITLE: [proposed blog title]
ANGLE: [3-5 sentences - what personal experience or data makes Michael's take unique]
TRENDING SIGNAL: [what you found in web search - specific subreddit, HN thread, trend, or event, be detailed and verbose with links to actual references]
SCORE: [1-10]
tasks.last_result:
# 📝 Blog Topic Ideas - April 18, 2026

---

## 1. TITLE: The Agentic AI Framework War: LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AutoGen in Production

**ANGLE:** Michael built 2 production multi-agent systems (enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report) and runs Agent Zero with 11 specialist agents. This post compares the 3 dominant frameworks from a practitioner's perspective - not benchmark scores, but real deployment experience: state management complexity, debugging multi-agent conversations, tool calling reliability under load, and operational overhead for production monitoring. Unlike generic comparison articles, this draws from hands-on experience building systems that actually run in production environments with real users.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Major comparison articles published March 2026 across multiple sources (Boolean Beyond, Medium Data Science Collective, Towards AI, DeployBase). The framework landscape has stratified into Tier 1 (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen) with genuine production deployments. Key sources:
- [Boolean Beyond: LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AutoGen](https://www.booleanbeyond.com/en/insights/langgraph-vs-crewai-vs-autogen-multi-agent-frameworks)
- [Medium Data Science Collective (Mar 12, 2026)](https://medium.com/data-science-collective/langgraph-vs-crewai-vs-autogen-which-ai-agent-framework-should-you-actually-use-in-2026-b8b2c84f1229)
- [Towards AI Production Guide (Mar 21, 2026)](https://pub.towardsai.net/langgraph-vs-crewai-vs-autogen-which-ai-agent-framework-should-your-enterprise-use-in-2026-3a9ebb407b09)
- [DeployBase: Multi-Agent Frameworks](https://deploybase.ai/articles/agentic-ai-frameworks)
- DeveloperWeek 2026 (Mar 5, 2026) covered interoperability and knowledge architecture as key themes

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 2. TITLE: The Last Mile of AI Transformation: Why Technical Capability Isn't Enough

**ANGLE:** Michael drove AI adoption from 28% to 100% department-wide with 2/3 building agentic AI. HBR's March 2026 article identifies the "last mile" problem as the primary obstacle - where technical capability meets organizational design. This post shares Michael's experience overcoming the gap between pilots and production-scale adoption: change management strategies, stakeholder alignment techniques, and practical frameworks for measuring transformation progress beyond technical metrics.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** HBR published "The Last Mile Problem Slowing AI Transformation" in March 2026. McKinsey's State of AI Trust (Mar 25, 2026) reports organizations moving beyond experimentation toward scaled deployment but struggling with consequences of failure as AI gains autonomy. MIT's Agentic AI in Engineering and Manufacturing paper (Mar 2026) identifies adoption challenges as the #1 industry concern. Key sources:
- [HBR: The Last Mile Problem](https://hbr.org/2026/03/the-last-mile-problem-slowing-ai-transformation)
- [McKinsey: State of AI Trust in 2026](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/tech-and-ai/our-insights/tech-forward/state-of-ai-trust-in-2026-shifting-to-the-agentic-era)
- [MIT.edu: Agentic AI in Engineering and Manufacturing (PDF, Mar 2026)](https://decode.mit.edu/assets/papers/2026_Edwards_Agentic_AI_in_Engineering_and_Manufacturing.pdf)
- [HBR Sponsored: Four Trends in AI Experimentation](https://hbr.org/sponsored/2026/03/four-trends-in-ai-experimentation-adoption-and-transformation)

**SCORE:** 9/10

---

## 3. TITLE: DORA Metrics Evolved: The Fifth Signal (Reliability) and What It Means for AI-Augmented Teams

**ANGLE:** DORA expanded to a 5-metric model in 2026, adding Reliability as the fifth signal. Honeycomb's April 10, 2026 analysis found AI drives both throughput AND instability - a finding that challenges the assumption that AI-only improves engineering metrics. Michael tracks Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%. This post explores how to measure and manage the throughput-stability paradox when AI tools accelerate development but introduce new failure modes, with practical frameworks for setting guardrails and monitoring reliability signals.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** DORA's 5-metric model launched in 2026 with Reliability as the new signal. Honeycomb published "Your Questions About AI Agents and Production Feedback Answered" (Apr 10, 2026) covering DORA report findings on AI driving both throughput and instability. Plandek released 2026 Engineering Productivity Benchmarks analyzing impact of AI-augmented engineering across Focus, Speed, Predictability, and Quality. Key sources:
- [Knowledgelib: Engineering Productivity Benchmarks 2026](https://knowledgelib.io/business/industry-benchmarks/engineering-productivity-benchmarks-2026/2026)
- [Honeycomb Blog (Apr 10, 2026)](https://www.honeycomb.io/blog/your-questions-about-ai-agents-production-feedback-answered)
- [Plandek: 2026 Engineering Productivity Benchmarks Report](https://plandek.com/resources/2026-software-delivery-benchmark-report/)
- [Typoapp: DORA Metrics for Engineering Leaders](https://typoapp.io/blog/dora-metrics-for-engineering-leaders)

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 4. TITLE: Local LLM Inference on a Budget: AMD Strix Halo and the Democratization of Enterprise AI

**ANGLE:** Michael runs a 128GB unified memory AMD Strix Halo AI server with ROCm/llama.cpp for local inference. With March 2026 articles covering Strix Halo mini PCs enabling 70B+ parameter LLMs on sub-$2K hardware, this post shares real setup costs, performance benchmarks, and organizational implications for data sovereignty. Connects to Michael's $100K+ cloud cost savings experience - showing how local inference reduces long-term licensing costs while maintaining compliance with data residency requirements.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Compute-Market published "Strix Halo Mini PCs for Local AI" (Mar 2026) highlighting 128GB unified LPDDR5X memory enabling 70B+ parameter LLMs. StarryHope published practical guide (Mar 2026). TinyComputers covered distilled reasoning on Strix Halo (Mar 29, 2026). LinkedIn deep dive by Deep Pellegrini on running 122B-parameter LLMs locally. GitHub repos for DreamServer and Framework-strix-halo-llm-setup gaining traction. Key sources:
- [Compute-Market: Strix Halo Mini PCs (Mar 2026)](https://www.compute-market.com/blog/strix-halo-mini-pc-local-ai-2026)
- [StarryHope: Practical Guide to Strix Halo (Mar 2026)](https://www.starryhope.com/minipcs/strix-halo-local-llm-inference-2026/)
- [TinyComputers: Distilled Reasoning on Strix Halo (Mar 29, 2026)](https://tinycomputers.io/posts/distilled-reasoning-on-strix-halo-qwen35-claude-thinking.html)
- [LinkedIn: Running 122B LLMs Locally](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/running-122b-parameter-llms-locally-amd-strix-halo-deep-pellegrini-33cjc)
- [GitHub: DreamServer](https://github.com/Light-Heart-Labs/DreamServer)

**SCORE:** 7/10

---

## 5. TITLE: Engineering Trust in the Age of Agentic AI

**ANGLE:** Forbes published "The Neuroscience Of Authority: Engineering Trust In Modern Leadership" (Apr 7, 2026) on trust-building through repetition and calm communication. Stack Overflow's July 2025 piece on aligned autonomy remains relevant. Michael's philosophy centers on trust-based autonomous teams. This post explores how to apply autonomy principles when AI agents are making decisions alongside humans - where does human accountability end and agent autonomy begin? Practical frameworks for establishing guardrails without creating bottlenecks.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Forbes Councils article "The Neuroscience Of Authority: Engineering Trust In Modern Leadership" (Apr 7, 2026). Stack Overflow published "Building stronger engineering teams with aligned autonomy" (Jul 2025) - still trending. GainHQ's March 2026 piece on engineering team structure emphasizes trust as glue between autonomy and alignment. Key sources:
- [Forbes Councils (Apr 7, 2026)](https://www.forbes.com/councils/forbescoachescouncil/2026/04/07/the-neuroscience-of-authority-engineering-trust-in-modern-leadership/)
- [Stack Overflow: Aligned Autonomy](https://stackoverflow.blog/2025/07/14/building-stronger-engineering-teams-with-aligned-autonomy/)
- [GainHQ: Engineering Team Structure 2026](https://gainhq.com/blog/engineering-team-structure/)

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 6. TITLE: Beyond Burnout: Building Sustainable Engineering Culture in APAC's High-Pressure Market

**ANGLE:** Singapore ranks first in global competitiveness yet faces a burnout crisis (IMD ranking, CNA TODAY reporting). Reeracoen published "Calm Work in 2026: Preventing Burnout Without Quitting" and MyCareersFuture covered "Beyond Burnout: Fix Work, Not Ourselves." Michael leads up to 14 cross-functional engineers. This post shares practical systems for preventing burnout while maintaining engineering excellence in APAC's competitive market - drawing from personal experience mentoring engineers through high-pressure cycles, including automation strategies that reduce cognitive load and create sustainable delivery rhythms.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Reeracoen Singapore published "Calm Work in 2026" article. CNA TODAY reported "Singapore faces crisis as worker burnout soars" with multiple surveys showing prevalence of workplace burnout. MyCareersFuture (gov.sg) published "Beyond Burnout: Fix Work, Not Ourselves in 2026." Instagram post (Mar 29, 2026) on learning Industry 4.0 with full-time job via small daily time slots. Key sources:
- [Reeracoen Singapore: Calm Work in 2026](https://www.reeracoen.sg/articles/calm-work-in-2026-preventing-burnout-without-quitting-your-job-in-singapore)
- [CNA TODAY: Singapore Burnout Crisis](https://theindependent.sg/singapore-faces-crisis-as-worker-burnout-soars/)
- [MyCareersFuture: Beyond Burnout](https://www.content.mycareersfuture.gov.sg/beyond-burnout-resolutions-fixing-work)

**SCORE:** 7/10

---

## 7. TITLE: AI-Accelerated Engineering: The Throughput-Stability Paradox

**ANGLE:** Honeycomb's April 10, 2026 analysis revealed AI drives both throughput AND instability - a finding that challenges the assumption that AI-only improves engineering metrics. Michael tracks SLO >98% and has deep observability experience (Datadog $70K savings). This post explores how to measure, monitor, and manage the trade-off between AI-accelerated delivery and system reliability - with practical frameworks for setting guardrails, implementing automated testing pipelines, and creating feedback loops that catch AI-generated defects before they reach production.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Honeycomb published "Your Questions About AI Agents and Production Feedback Answered" (Apr 10, 2026) covering DORA report findings on AI driving both throughput and instability. BCG's April 3, 2026 publication "AI Will Reshape More Jobs Than It Replaces" discusses productivity gains alongside system complexity. Harness published load testing best practices guide (Mar 27, 2026). Key sources:
- [Honeycomb Blog (Apr 10, 2026)](https://www.honeycomb.io/blog/your-questions-about-ai-agents-production-feedback-answered)
- [BCG: AI Will Reshape More Jobs (Apr 3, 2026)](https://www.bcg.com/publications/2026/ai-will-reshape-more-jobs-than-it-replaces)
- [Harness Blog: Load Testing Best Practices (Mar 27, 2026)](https://www.harness.io/blog-category/engineering-blog)

**SCORE:** 8/10
